News

1/9/2004 MSI Announces its "Genomic Sciences Summer 2004 Internship" for High School Juniors and Seniors
10/29/2003 Dr. Roger Brent, Molecular Sciences Institute's President and Research Director, is honored with the Gabbay Award
10/2/2003 The Molecular Science Institute Awarded its First United States Patent
9/24/2003 The Molecular Sciences Institute's "Alpha Project" supports Systems Biology Markup Language
9/24/2003 Dan Gillespie, revered mathematical chemist, joins MSI's efforts to model biological systems.
9/8/2003 "Towards an Anthropology of Biology: Genomics and Citizenship" : CGEC course debuts at the University of California, Berkeley
4/15/2003 MONOD Adds Features, Developers and Momentum
12/13/2002 Announcing the First Annual Alpha Project Research Symposium: December 13, 2002
12/10/2002 Sydney Brenner receives the Nobel Prize in Stockholm today
10/7/2002 Sydney Brenner, Molecular Sciences founder, is awarded the Nobel Prize
9/26/2002 MSI and DARPA forge new tool for collaborative bioresearch
8/5/2002 MSI Chosen as a Center of Excellence in Genomic Sciences by the National Institutes of Health and Awarded a $15.5M Grant for the Alpha Project
5/14/2002 Researchers Link Cell Proliferation to Cell Death
4/15/2002 MSI Evolution Project Funded by the National Science Foundation
12/13/2001 Researchers Discover Why Fate of Daughter Yeast Cell Differs From Its Mother; Early Milestone For Understanding How Single Cells Work
10/31/2001 The MSI Announces Changes to Board of Trustees
7/24/2001 The MSI Appoints Roger Brent President and Chief Executive Officer

2/24/2003 "The Alpha Project: Coalition Sets Ambitious Goal to Model a Signaling Pathway", The Scientist (free registration required)
9/10/2002 MSI President Roger Brent Ph.D. Featured in "Inside Genomics", Incyte Genomics Corp
8/30/2002 "Studies of stickleback and yeast win new US government funding", Genome News Network
8/21/2002 "Berkeley Lab Wins $15.5 Million Grant", Berkeley Daily Planet
8/19/2002 "Biotech Yeast Project Explores the Mechanics of Schmoo", San Francisco Chronicle
